summary.intent: Complete a Codex-driven TDD review loop on PR #669 of fogodev/ars-ui, fixing combobox selection bugs identified by Codex review and resolving all review threads.
summary.outcome: Two rounds of Codex review were completed: first clearing three threads about selection sync (Clear, stale selection pruning, custom value commits), then fixing two new findings (strict empty Enter should revert to selection, blur should not mutate disabled/readonly comboboxes). All fixes were committed and pushed with passing tests, clippy, fmt, and coverage.

repo: Combobox selection logic in ars-components uses a shared 'controlled selection sync path' — all mutation points (Clear, custom commits, pruning) must go through this path to keep controlled selection, selection_state, selected rendering, and hidden input serialization in agreement.
The combobox has a disabled_and_readonly_guards pattern that must be applied to Blur event handling to prevent unintended value commits or reverts.
The review loop is driven by @codex review comments on the PR; Codex signals active review with 👀 reaction and posts threads when findings are ready.
CI includes coverage, feature, and release jobs that run after core checks; these may still be pending while early jobs are green.
Strict empty Enter must use the same revert path as other strict commits, not merely close the dropdown.

code.finding: Region around line 790-825 inspected for selection sync logic during first fix pass.
Region around line 1905-1935 inspected, likely contains event dispatch or transition logic.
Region around line 2450-2535 inspected for Blur/Enter handling and commit logic.
Region inspected for guard patterns and disabled/readonly checks.
Region inspected, likely contains collection refresh and pruning logic.
Region inspected for make_service, collection, and send_event function signatures.

workflow: TDD loop: add failing regression tests first, confirm they fail on current code, then patch — ensures the fix actually targets the reported behavior.
Gate before commit: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo llvm-cov, and git diff --check all run together as a pre-commit gate.
After a behavior-changing patch followed by a cosmetic cleanup, re-run the full gate from the final worktree state so coverage evidence reflects the shipping code.
Codex review threads are replied to with the fix commit SHA and resolved via GraphQL mutation; unresolved thread count is polled before triggering the next @codex review.
Polling cadence of ~2-minute sleeps used while waiting for Codex to finish reviewing; 👀 reaction indicates active review, absence of threads means no actionable findings yet.

friction: Codex took multiple 2-minute polling cycles (5+ waits) before producing new findings, requiring extended idle monitoring.
The blur guard branch needed a cosmetic cleanup pass after rustfmt reformatted it awkwardly, adding an extra round of checks.
CI coverage/feature/release jobs were still pending after core checks completed, meaning full CI green status was not immediately confirmed.
open_items: Full CI green status (coverage, feature, release jobs) was still pending at the time of the final push — outcome of those jobs not confirmed in transcript.
The Codex review cycle was still potentially ongoing at transcript end; further Codex findings could require additional fix passes.
